mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 4.5% indicates weakening labor market conditions, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal a contraction in real economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce negative returns in > 50% of cases where both INDPRO and HOUST decline, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% prompts expectations of monetary tightening, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during active tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: The signal fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases where GDPC1 grows above 3%, or the 60d return distribution does not significantly outperform baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in residential construction (RSAFS) below $490 billion indicates weak demand for housing, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS falls below 490, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) and decrease in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls, or the coefficient for the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 105 signals a supply-side shock, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of economic uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105, or the signal's IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
